level_signals: 4–6+ years in Sales Operations, Revenue Operations, or GTM Strategy
Leads or co-leads weekly forecast calls with Sales leadership
Influences senior stakeholders without formal authority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4–6+ years in Sales Operations, Revenue Operations, or GTM Strategy.
Experience supporting Direct Sales Teams and/or Channel organizations.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to turn data into decisions.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where none exists.
Excellent executive communication — written, verbal, and storytelling.
Ability to influence senior stakeholders without formal authority.
High ownership mentality: you see problems and fix them.
Detail-oriented but able to zoom out to the bigger picture.
Strong experience with Salesforce, Tableau, Outreach, Clay, Scratchpad, and ZoomInfo.
Ability to partner effectively with Business systems and analytics teams.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity working alongside tools like Anaplan, routing platforms (Leandata), or forecasting tools (Clari).

scope.surface: Downmarket (Commercial and Small Business) sales motion — pipeline health, forecast discipline, deal inspection frameworks
core_responsibilities: Define and enforce opportunity stage entry and exit criteria
Establish deal inspection frameworks by segment and deal size
Identify and eliminate poorly qualified pipeline, stalled deals, inflated close dates
Own forecast methodology and call discipline across segments
Standardize forecast categories (Commit, Best Case, Pipeline, etc.)
Lead or co-lead weekly forecast calls with Sales leadership
Track and surface forecast bias, slippage patterns, and risk signals
Design and run pipeline councils by segment
Translate execution issues into process improvements and system enforcement
Define and own core pipeline and forecast KPIs

green_flags: Clear, specific role scope — 'revenue execution role — not reporting support and not an embedded business partner'
Concrete list of responsibilities tied to measurable outcomes (forecast accuracy, pipeline conversion)
Role reports directly to Director of Business Operations — clear reporting line
Encourages candidates who don't meet every qualification to apply
Specific toolstack named (Salesforce, Tableau, Outreach, Clay, Scratchpad, ZoomInfo) — transparency about environment
red_flags: Office time requirement unclear despite specifying Austin or Tempe offices — remote eligibility ambiguous
Role title includes 'Manager' but no people management signals — could be confusing around seniority expectations

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years of experience range and responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'SaaS experience strongly preferred' and company description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as KPIs in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from 'opportunity to be based in our Austin or Tempe offices' language)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from office-based language; no explicit hybrid/remote policy stated)
ambiguities: Location type is ambiguous: JD says 'opportunity to be based in our Austin or Tempe offices' which could mean hybrid or fully onsite; treated as hybrid here but could be onsite.
'SaaS experience strongly preferred' sits between a hard and soft requirement — 'strongly preferred' language treated as soft (nice-to-have) rather than hard.
Years range '7–10 years of total professional experience' paired with '4–6+ years in Sales/Rev Ops' — the '+' on 4-6 adds ambiguity about upper bound; captured minimum only.
